title: What 84 Days of Money Work Does (and Doesn't Do)
subtitle: A short reading on what structured money mindset work actually changes, and what it leaves for you to do every day after
excerpt: A completion reading for the end of the 84-day arc. It names what twelve weeks of tapping, journalling, and energy work shifts, and what it does not claim to do, so the work that follows is grounded in what is real.
sourceNotes: Original reading written for homemade.education. Synthesised from principles across MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

practice.whenToUse: Read at the close of a structured money programme, or when you want to understand what the inner work has actually done and what daily life continues to ask of you.

content.text: Eighty-four days of consistent inner work changes the baseline. The fear that used to sit just below every financial decision gets quieter. The guilt around spending, the panic around bills, the old sense that money is always about to run out — these patterns lose their grip. They do not disappear, but they stop running the show.

The identity shift is real. Who you think you are around money matters: whether you are someone money comes to easily, whether you deserve a full account, whether you can make a large decision without collapsing into doubt. That belief changes. And identity is sticky. Once you hold yourself as the kind of person who handles money well, you tend to handle money well.

The relationship between money and emotion changes too. Financial numbers stop feeling like a verdict on your worth. Good months stop producing guilt; harder months stop producing shame. Money becomes more information and less judgement.

Mindset work does not generate income by itself. A cleared belief and an unchanged business model produce a clearer-headed person with the same business model. The inner work creates the conditions for better decisions. The better decisions still need making.

It does not inoculate permanently. Old patterns return under stress. The person who completed 84 days of tapping will still have moments of scarcity thinking after a hard month. The difference is speed of recovery, not immunity.

It does not replace skill. Knowing how to price, how to invest, how to read a cash flow, how to negotiate — none of that is substituted by an aligned identity. The cleared beliefs make the skill-building easier. They do not skip it.

The work does not end at Day 84. What ends is the structure. What continues is the practice of returning to what you now know when the noise starts again — which it will. Tapping when a fear surfaces. Journalling when a pattern reappears. Choosing the generous interpretation of a financial event before the fearful one.
Abundance as identity is not a destination. It is a practice you now know how to do. The 84 days gave you the fluency. What you do with it is yours.
Written for homemade.education. Draws on principles from MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
